message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes.

FINDING P10-CI-1:
require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest CI run's conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run from an earlier commit certified a newer untested HEAD (fix-regression of CI-1's gate). Fix: fetch headSha; compute head_sha=git rev-parse origin/<branch> (fallback <branch>); if run_sha != head_sha exit 1 (CI for this commit not complete); else require completed/success. VERIFY by reading the script and reasoning: does it fail closed when the latest run is for a different/older SHA? does it still pass on a genuinely-green current HEAD? Test: test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists asserts headSha + rev-parse present. Edge: shallow clone / origin ref missing (fallback to local <branch>).

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest"; gh for CI). Judge ONLY P10-CI-1. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding P10-CI-1. Let me start by examining the script and tests.

content.content:
d890ed3 fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
17b4eb5 docs(hardening): pass-9 merged (#170); open pass 10
a49b3f6 Merge pull request #170 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-9
edbb576 docs(hardening): record pass 9 — actual-CI-execution + fix-regression each caught a HIGH
22cbcad fix(hardening-p9): wire ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS into the capture hook so [REDACTED] actually scrubs (P9-PII-1 concern)
harden/pass-10

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Pre-merge CI gate (#CI-1) — compensating control for an unenforceable required status check.
3	#
4	# Branch protection / required status checks are UNAVAILABLE on this repo's plan: it is private on
5	# a free tier, so `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/branches/main/protection` returns HTTP 403
6	# ("Upgrade to GitHub Pro or make this repository public"). GitHub therefore does NOT block merging
7	# a PR whose CI is red — the `all-checks` fan-in job is computed but nothing consumes it as a gate.
8	# That is the exact mechanism by which CI went silently red across several hardening passes (merged
9	# via --admin / un-gated). Making the repo public to enable protection is out of scope (it would
10	# publish the committed transcripts); upgrading the plan is an operator/billing decision.
11	#
12	# Until one of those changes, THIS is the gate: it exits non-zero unless the latest `ci` run for the
13	# branch concluded success. Run it before every merge and refuse to merge on failure:
14	#
15	#   scripts/require_green_ci.sh [branch]      # defaults to the current branch
16	#   scripts/require_green_ci.sh && gh pr merge <n> --merge --delete-branch
17	set -euo pipefail
18	
19	branch="${1:-$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)}"
20	# The SHA the green run MUST match (#P10-CI-1): checking only the latest run's conclusion let a
21	# STALE green run (from an earlier commit) certify a newer, untested HEAD — the gate must bind the
22	# green result to the exact commit being merged, and fail closed if no completed run exists for it.
23	head_sha="$(git rev-parse "origin/$branch" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse "$branch")"
24	read -r status conclusion run_sha < <(
25	  gh run list --branch "$branch" --workflow ci.yml --limit 1 \
26	    --json status,conclusion,headSha \
27	    -q '.[0].status + " " + (.[0].conclusion // "none") + " " + (.[0].headSha // "none")'
28	)
29	echo "latest ci run for '$branch': status=$status conclusion=$conclusion sha=$run_sha (HEAD=$head_sha)"
30	if [ "$run_sha" != "$head_sha" ]; then
31	  echo "::error::latest CI run is for $run_sha but '$branch' HEAD is $head_sha — CI for this commit has not completed; do NOT merge (#P10-CI-1)." >&2
32	  exit 1
33	fi
34	if [ "$status" = "completed" ] && [ "$conclusion" = "success" ]; then
35	  echo "CI is green for the current HEAD — safe to merge."
36	  exit 0
37	fi
38	echo "::error::CI for '$branch'@$head_sha is not green (status=$status conclusion=$conclusion) — do NOT merge (#CI-1)." >&2
39	exit 1
40

content.text: The script looks correct. Let me examine the test and verify it discriminates.

content.content:
339	def test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists():
340	    # #CI-1: branch protection is unavailable on this plan, so the all-checks job is not enforced
341	    # by the platform. The compensating control must exist: a pre-merge CI-gate script + a policy
342	    # doc, and the workflow comment must be HONEST that enforcement is procedural, not platform.
343	    script = _ROOT / "scripts" / "require_green_ci.sh"
344	    assert script.exists(), "missing pre-merge CI gate script (#CI-1)"
345	    body = script.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
346	    assert "gh run list" in body and "conclusion" in body and "exit 1" in body
347	    policy = _ROOT / "docs" / "MERGE-POLICY.md"
348	    assert policy.exists() and "branch protection" in policy.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
349	    ci = _ci_text()
350	    assert "#CI-1" in ci, "all-checks comment does not flag the unenforced-gate caveat"
351	    # #P10-CI-1: the gate must bind the green run to the exact commit (headSha == HEAD), else a
352	    # stale green run from an earlier commit certifies a newer untested HEAD.
353	    assert "headSha" in body, "CI gate does not compare the run's headSha to HEAD (#P10-CI-1)"
354	    assert "rev-parse" in body, "CI gate does not resolve the branch HEAD SHA to compare"
355	
356	
357	def test_numeric_env_parsing_names_the_bad_var():
358	    # #P9-MO-2: numeric env vars must be parsed via the shared helpers that name the offending var,
359	    # not bare int()/float() that raise an opaque "invalid literal" at import. Verify the helpers
360	    # AND that seam.py/checkpoint.py no longer carry bare numeric parses of os.environ.
361	    import importlib
362	
363	    ep = importlib.import_module("assay_engine._envparse")
364	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="ASSAY_X must be an integer"):
365	        import os as _os
366	
367	        _os.environ["ASSAY_X"] = "not-an-int"
368	        try:
369	            ep.int_env("ASSAY_X", "5")
370	        finally:
371	            del _os.environ["ASSAY_X"]
372	    seam = (_ROOT / "src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
373	    chk = (_ROOT / "src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
374	    import re as _re
375	
376	    for src, name in ((seam, "seam.py"), (chk, "checkpoint.py")):
377	        assert "_envparse import" in src, f"{name} does not use the shared env-parse helpers"
378	        # no bare int(/float( wrapping an os.environ/_env(...) read
379	        assert not _re.search(r"\b(?:int|float)\(\s*(?:os\.environ|_env\()", src), (
380	            f"{name} still has a bare numeric parse of an env var (#P9-MO-2)"
381	        )
382	
383	
384	def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
385	    # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
386	    # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.
387	    import re
388	
389	    text = _ci_text()
390	    uses = re.findall(r"uses:\s*(\S+)", text)
391	    assert uses, "no actions found"
392	    for ref in uses:
393	        _, _, pin = ref.partition("@")
394	        assert re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{40}", pin), f"action {ref!r} not pinned to a commit SHA"
395	
396	
397	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
398	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.

content.text: Test discriminates. Now let me reason through the script's logic for fail-closed behavior and edge cases. Let me verify the bash logic handles the key scenarios.

input.command:
cd /tmp && cat > t.sh <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
# simulate the gate's core comparison logic with injected values
test_case() {
  local run_sha="$1" head_sha="$2" status="$3" conclusion="$4" desc="$5"
  if [ "$run_sha" != "$head_sha" ]; then echo "[$desc] -> EXIT 1 (sha mismatch)"; return; fi
  if [ "$status" = "completed" ] && [ "$conclusion" = "success" ]; then echo "[$desc] -> EXIT 0 (green)"; return; fi
  echo "[$desc] -> EXIT 1 (not green)"
}
test_case "abc" "abc" "completed" "success" "green current HEAD"
test_case "old" "new" "completed" "success" "STALE green run (older sha)"
test_case "abc" "abc" "in_progress" "none" "running for HEAD"
test_case "abc" "abc" "completed" "failure" "red for HEAD"
test_case "none" "new" "none" "none" "no runs at all (headSha=none)"
EOF
bash t.sh

content.text: All scenarios behave correctly. Let me check the syntax validity and one subtle edge: what if both run_sha and head_sha resolve to "none" (no origin ref, no local branch, no runs).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && bash -n scripts/require_green_ci.sh && echo "syntax OK"
# edge: git rev-parse failing entirely. With set -e, if both origin and local fail, command substitution -> set -e? 
# head_sha="$(git rev-parse origin/X 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse X)" — if local also fails, rev-parse prints error to stderr and exits non-zero, killing the script under set -e.
# Test: nonexistent branch resolution behavior
git rev-parse "origin/definitely-no-such-branch" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse "definitely-no-such-branch" 2>&1 | head -2; echo "exit=$?"
input.description: Check syntax and rev-parse failure edge

content.content:
syntax OK
origin/definitely-no-such-branch
fatal: ambiguous argument 'definitely-no-such-branch': unknown revision or path not in the working tree.
Use '--' to separate paths from revisions, like this:
exit=0

content.text:
Note the exit=0 there is from `head` in the pipe. The key point: if both rev-parse calls fail, `set -e` causes the script to abort (non-zero exit) before reaching the merge — which is fail-closed (the operator won't get exit 0). That's acceptable behavior for the gate. Let me confirm the shallow-clone fallback path is sound: the fallback to local `<branch>` is correct when `origin/<branch>` is missing.

The script's design is correct. Let me do a final check that the full supply_chain test file passes (no collateral breakage).

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_supply_chain.py 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Run full supply chain test file

content.text:
All checks pass. My assessment:

**Appropriateness (root cause):** The fix correctly targets the actual mechanism of P10-CI-1. The original gate read only `status`/`conclusion` of the latest run, so a green run from an older commit certified a newer untested HEAD. The fix binds the green result to the exact commit by fetching `headSha` from `gh run list` and comparing against `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` (with local `<branch>` fallback). Right layer (the gate script itself), sound approach.

**Completeness / fail-closed:** Verified by simulation across all relevant scenarios:
- Green current HEAD → exit 0 (still passes genuinely-green HEAD) ✓
- Stale green run (older sha) → exit 1 ✓ (the bug)
- In-progress for HEAD → exit 1 ✓
- Red for HEAD → exit 1 ✓
- No runs at all (`headSha`=none vs real HEAD) → exit 1 (sha mismatch) ✓ fail-closed
- SHA check is ordered BEFORE the status/conclusion check, so the bind happens first.
- Edge (both `origin/<branch>` and local `<branch>` rev-parse fail): `set -e` aborts the script non-zero → still fail-closed.

**Discrimination:** Reverting the script to the conclusion-only version makes `test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists` FAIL on the `"headSha" in body` assertion. Test passes on the real fix. The test asserts both `headSha` and `rev-parse` presence as required by the finding.

Minor note (not a gap): the test is a text-presence assertion on the script body, not a behavioral execution of the gate against a real stale run. It would not catch a future logic regression that kept the tokens but reordered/inverted the comparison. This matches the established pattern of this repo's gate test (which already only greps for `gh run list`/`conclusion`/`exit 1`) and the finding explicitly specifies the headSha+rev-parse presence assertion. The behavioral logic is nonetheless correct as verified by direct simulation.

input.rationale: The fix correctly addresses P10-CI-1's root cause: the prior gate read only the latest run's status/conclusion, letting a stale green run from an earlier commit certify a newer untested HEAD. The fix (scripts/require_green_ci.sh:23-33) fetches headSha via `gh run list --json ...,headSha`, computes head_sha=`git rev-parse origin/<branch>` with a local `<branch>` fallback, and exits 1 when run_sha != head_sha — BEFORE the status/conclusion check. I validated the comparison logic by simulation: green current HEAD -> exit 0; stale green (older sha) -> exit 1; in-progress/red for HEAD -> exit 1; no runs at all (headSha=none vs real HEAD) -> exit 1. It still passes a genuinely-green current HEAD. The shallow-clone/missing-origin edge is handled by the rev-parse fallback, and if both rev-parse calls fail, `set -e` aborts non-zero — fail-closed throughout. Syntax checks clean (bash -n). The regression test DISCRIMINATES: reverting the script to the conclusion-only version makes test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists fail on the `'headSha' in body` assertion; it passes on the real fix. Full tests/test_supply_chain.py: 42 passed.
concerns: Minor (not a gap, consistent with repo pattern and finding spec): the regression test only asserts textual presence of 'headSha' and 'rev-parse' in the script body; it does not execute the gate against a simulated stale run, so a future logic regression that retains the tokens but inverts/reorders the SHA comparison would not be caught. The behavioral correctness was verified here by direct simulation.

content.text:
Verdict: CONFIRMED for P10-CI-1.

The fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/require_green_ci.sh` is both appropriate and complete:
- Binds the green CI result to the exact HEAD commit by fetching `headSha` and comparing against `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` (local fallback), with the SHA check ordered before the status/conclusion check.
- Fails closed in every adversarial scenario I simulated (stale green, no runs, missing ref).
- The regression test `tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_merge_gate_compensating_control_exists` discriminates (fails on revert, passes on the fix).

One minor non-blocking concern: the test is a text-presence assertion rather than a behavioral execution of the gate, consistent with the existing repo pattern and the finding's specification.
